repair_candidate.summary: Repair #90867 so the backend-delegated `/export-session` warning applies cleanly to current main while preserving @xydigit-sj's contributor branch and attribution.
repair_candidate.pr_title: fix(auto-reply): warn on backend-delegated session exports
repair_candidate.pr_body:
What Problem This Solves

`/export-session` can currently produce a successful HTML export for backend-delegated sessions that only contains OpenClaw-side user messages, which makes the export look complete even though assistant/tool/usage data lives in the backend transcript. This carries forward #90867 by @xydigit-sj and the linked #90844 behavior report.

Why This Change Was Made

The existing contributor PR is the right narrow path, but its patch no longer applies cleanly to current `main`. Repair the contributor branch against current `main`, add a small typed helper that detects non-empty transcripts whose message entries are all `role: "user"`, pass a warning through `SessionData`, render it escaped in the exported HTML header area, and include the same warning in the command reply.

User Impact

Users exporting CLI/ACP backend-delegated sessions get an explicit warning that assistant replies, tool calls, and usage data are not included in the OpenClaw export. Normal mixed transcripts remain unchanged.

Evidence

- Current main lacks the warning path in `src/auto-reply/reply/commands-export-session.ts` and `src/auto-reply/reply/export-html/template.js`.
- The #90867 patch no longer applies cleanly to current main, so the contributor branch needs a rebase/repair before merge.
- Run `pnpm test src/auto-reply/reply/commands-export-session.test.ts` and `pnpm check:changed` after repair.

Credit

Based on and credited to @xydigit-sj's source PR: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/90867.
credit_notes: Repair the existing contributor PR rather than replacing it: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/90867 by @xydigit-sj.
Keep @xydigit-sj as the credited author/source in the PR body or repair note; the fix idea and tests come from #90867.
